message:
Extend SelectPlannerDirective with probe-key metadata

Groundwork for richer planner → codegen communication of index probe
shape. Right now the planner hands codegen only the leading index
column name; this commit adds four new optional fields to
SelectPlannerDirective so the planner can eventually describe the
probe key itself (which may be an expression, not a column) and the
equality/range bounds it intends to drive the probe with:

- index_key_label: Option<String>
    Human-readable rendering of the probe key, defaulting to the
    column name for simple column indexes. For expression indexes
    where we know the index exists but have not yet recovered the
    underlying column, we emit "(expr)" so codegen diagnostics stay
    informative instead of showing "(none)".
- index_key_is_expression: bool
    True when the directive was produced from an expression index
    (index_name present, index_column absent). Lets lowering paths
    branch on "expression probe vs column probe" without guessing
    from string contents.
- index_equality_target: Option<PlannerIndexRangeBound>
    Equality probe bound placeholder. Always None in this commit; the
    field is in place so future planner work can fill it in without a
    second schema churn.
- index_range_target: Option<PlannerIndexRangeTarget>
    Range probe description with optional lower/upper bounds, each
    carrying its label and inclusive flag. Also always None in this
    commit.

Two new types are introduced in fsqlite-vdbe::codegen to describe
those bounds:

- PlannerIndexRangeBound { label, inclusive }
- PlannerIndexRangeTarget { lower, upper }

Both derive Debug/Clone/PartialEq/Eq and the range target derives
Default so planners can populate only the half they have.

Connection::build_select_planner_directive is updated to produce the
new fields from what it already knows: when a named index with no
resolved column is chosen (expression index), it emits
index_key_label = Some("(expr)") and index_key_is_expression = true.
Column indexes get their column name echoed into index_key_label for
consistency. The equality/range target fields are left as None for
now — the planner does not yet expose its bound information to this
layer and we do not want to guess at it.

Existing codegen tests that construct SelectPlannerDirective literals
are updated to include the new fields (all None / false for the full
table scan and rowid lookup cases, Some(column_name) / false for the
index equality cases that already had index_column set). No runtime
codegen behavior changes.

Also adds a small helper on the fsqlite-vfs host_fs module:

  pub fn copy_file(from: &Path, to: &Path) -> Result<()>

which creates the destination's parent directory (if any) and then
delegates to std::fs::copy. This mirrors the existing write_file /
remove_file helpers and gives upcoming snapshot/rollback code a
single copy entry point that handles the "parent doesn't exist yet"
case the same way they already handle it for writes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -265,6 +265,22 @@ impl PlannerSelectAccessKind {
     }
 }
 
+/// Planner-facing description of a single bound in an index range probe.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
+pub struct PlannerIndexRangeBound {
+    /// Human-readable rendering of the bound expression.
+    pub label: String,
+    /// Whether the bound is inclusive (<= / >=) or exclusive (< / >).
+    pub inclusive: bool,
+}
+
+/// Planner-facing description of an index range probe target.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
+pub struct PlannerIndexRangeTarget {
+    pub lower: Option<PlannerIndexRangeBound>,
+    pub upper: Option<PlannerIndexRangeBound>,
+}
+
 /// Planner-produced directive for a single-table SELECT lowering path.
 #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
 pub struct SelectPlannerDirective {
@@ -280,6 +296,14 @@ pub struct SelectPlannerDirective {
     pub index_name: Option<String>,
     /// Leading index column the planner expects to drive the probe.
     pub index_column: Option<String>,
+    /// Label for the probe key (column name or expression label).
+    pub index_key_label: Option<String>,
+    /// Whether the planner expects the index key to be an expression.
+    pub index_key_is_expression: bool,
+    /// Equality probe target (if any).
+    pub index_equality_target: Option<PlannerIndexRangeBound>,
+    /// Range probe target (if any).
+    pub index_range_target: Option<PlannerIndexRangeTarget>,
     /// Whether the planner expects a covering-index lowering.
     pub covering: bool,
     /// Access-path family lowering should consume.
@@ -18770,6 +18794,10 @@ mod tests {
                 table_name: "messages".to_owned(),
                 index_name: Some("sqlite_autoindex_messages_1".to_owned()),
                 index_column: Some("conversation_id".to_owned()),
+                index_key_label: Some("conversation_id".to_owned()),
+                index_key_is_expression: false,
+                index_equality_target: None,
+                index_range_target: None,
                 covering: false,
                 access_kind: PlannerSelectAccessKind::IndexEquality,
             }),
@@ -20845,6 +20873,10 @@ mod tests {
                 table_name: "t".to_owned(),
                 index_name: None,
                 index_column: None,
+                index_key_label: None,
+                index_key_is_expression: false,
+                index_equality_target: None,
+                index_range_target: None,
                 covering: false,
                 access_kind: PlannerSelectAccessKind::FullTableScan,
             }),
@@ -20885,6 +20917,10 @@ mod tests {
                 table_name: "t".to_owned(),
                 index_name: None,
                 index_column: None,
+                index_key_label: None,
+                index_key_is_expression: false,
+                index_equality_target: None,
+                index_range_target: None,
                 covering: false,
                 access_kind: PlannerSelectAccessKind::RowidLookup,
             }),
@@ -20920,6 +20956,10 @@ mod tests {
                 table_name: "t".to_owned(),
                 index_name: Some("idx_t_b".to_owned()),
                 index_column: Some("b".to_owned()),
+                index_key_label: Some("b".to_owned()),
+                index_key_is_expression: false,
+                index_equality_target: None,
+                index_range_target: None,
                 covering: true,
                 access_kind: PlannerSelectAccessKind::IndexEquality,
             }),
